retrieved.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
You can end your studies in the following ways:

- successfully completing your degree programme and graduating
- closing one or multiple degree programme(s) (formerly called "exmatriculation")

Closing degree programmes is possible all year round. This is how you proceed:

1. Deregister from all courses with continous assessment and examinations. This is possible via u:space during the deregistration period. Afterwards, please contact the lecturers directly. Closing your studies does not qualify as an important reason for the cancellation of an examination (info only available in German).
2. In case, return books to the University Library or institute libraries. You can only withdraw (= close all degree programmes at the University of Vienna) if you have not borrowed any books.
3. If you close a bachelor-/diploma or master programme, please message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/category/stuz) and specify which degree programme you want to close. If you want to close a doctoral/PhD programme, please contact [doktorat.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).
4. After a few days, we will inform you about the processing of your application. Check the changed status of your degree programme in u:space at Studies > Study overview. You can download your confirmation of withdrawal at Personal matters > My documents.

retrieved.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
retrieved.text:
The admission to the degree programme expires and your degree programme is closed if:

- You have successfully completed your degree programme. An admission for the final administrative steps carried out by the StudiesServicecenter (SSC) is not required. The admission ends with the date of the last examination.
- You do not pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee. You can also not pay on purpose and thereby discontinue your studies and withdraw from the university.
- If you have paid at another Austrian university/college of teacher education ("Pädagogische Hochschule") and do not re-enroll your studies at the University of Vienna.
- If you do not achieve the [minimum number of credits](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/). This applies for admissions starting from winter semester 2022/23.

You can see that your degree programme has been closed in u:space at Studies > Study overview.

The [resumption of studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/) is possible within the corresponding period, provided you fulfil all requirements for the degree programme. By resuming your studies you are transferred to the current curriculum. Therefore, it may be possible that not all examinations you have already completed are recognised.

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: What do you need to consider?
retrieved.text: If you apply for a leave of absence, this may have the following consequences: / - Effects on your studies - The admission to the degree programme remains valid. You can continue to use the u:account services and the library. - The semester count is suspended (including the [minimum number of credits](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/)). - You will only receive the student record sheet and no confirmation of enrolment. - The u:card cannot be validated as a student ID for the semester on leave. - A leave of absence does not stop an automatic transfer to a new curriculum. - A leave of absence before the start of the semester can be granted for one or two semesters. In the case of a leave of absence during the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. The leave of absence ends automatically. Submit a new application for the following semester within the application deadline to continue your leave of absence. - Depending on the reason for leave of absence and the documents submitted, the application will be approved for one or two semesters. If the reason for your leave of absence continues to exist after this (e.g. due to caring for your own children), you can submit a new application. - Effects on examinations and courses Taking examinations, submitting an application for recognition, submitting academic work for assessment and attending

retrieved.text:
You have the option of [closing your studies](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/) and resuming them at a later date. Consider this option especially if it is likely that your studies will be interrupted for a long time. Please inform yourself in advance about possible conditions for [resuming your studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/). For master programmes with an admission procedure and the bachelor programme in Psychology, you must successfully complete the entrance exam/selection procedure again. For the bachelor programme in Sport Science and the teaching subject in Sports and Physical Education, you may have to take the [sporting aptitude admission test](https://lehre-schmelz.univie.ac.at/zulassung-zum-studium/ergaenzungspruefung/) again. Please contact the [SSC Sport Science](https://lehre-schmelz.univie.ac.at/studienservicecenter-und-studienprogrammleitung/) for more information.

As an alternative to a leave of absence, you may apply for a [remission of the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/). Please note the respective application deadlines and reasons for remission.

retrieved.title: Minimum number of credits
heading_path: FAQs about the minimum number of credits
retrieved.text: will lose your admission to the program if you do not achieve the minimum number of credits by the deadline. - Do I also have to complete the minimum number of credits when I resume my studies? Students of all bachelor's and diploma programmes who begin or [resume](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/) their studies in the winter semester of 2022/2023 or later must obtain the minimum number of credits. The minimum number of credits must be obtained once within four semesters. If you interrupt your studies (again) during these four semesters, the ECTS credits and semesters are counted after you resume your studies. However, once the minimum number of credits has been obtained, it does not have to be obtained again for this degree programme. For example: You started your studies in the winter semester of 2022/23, studied for 2 semesters and obtained 12 ECTS credits. You interrupt your studies in the following semester. You resume your studies in the summer semester of 2024. You still have 2 semesters to obtain the missing 4 ECTS credits - i.e. until one month after the end of the fourth semester at the latest. If you interrupt your studies again after that, you do not have to prove again that you have obtained the minimum number of credits in this study programme again. If you do not take a certain number of exams, it does not have any consequences for you personally in terms of study law. Students can have various reasons why they temporarily take fewer exams: gainful employment, family duties and care obligations. - If you are not able to take exams for a certain time due to personal reasons, you can apply for a [leave of absence](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission/leave-of-absence/). - Also students enrolled in more than one degree programme often take fewer exams. reranked.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
You can end your studies in the following ways:

- successfully completing your degree programme and graduating
- closing one or multiple degree programme(s) (formerly called "exmatriculation")

Closing degree programmes is possible all year round. This is how you proceed:

1. Deregister from all courses with continous assessment and examinations. This is possible via u:space during the deregistration period. Afterwards, please contact the lecturers directly. Closing your studies does not qualify as an important reason for the cancellation of an examination (info only available in German).
2. In case, return books to the University Library or institute libraries. You can only withdraw (= close all degree programmes at the University of Vienna) if you have not borrowed any books.
3. If you close a bachelor-/diploma or master programme, please message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/category/stuz) and specify which degree programme you want to close. If you want to close a doctoral/PhD programme, please contact [doktorat.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).
4. After a few days, we will inform you about the processing of your application. Check the changed status of your degree programme in u:space at Studies > Study overview. You can download your confirmation of withdrawal at Personal matters > My documents.

reranked.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
reranked.text:
The admission to the degree programme expires and your degree programme is closed if:

- You have successfully completed your degree programme. An admission for the final administrative steps carried out by the StudiesServicecenter (SSC) is not required. The admission ends with the date of the last examination.
- You do not pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee. You can also not pay on purpose and thereby discontinue your studies and withdraw from the university.
- If you have paid at another Austrian university/college of teacher education ("Pädagogische Hochschule") and do not re-enroll your studies at the University of Vienna.
- If you do not achieve the [minimum number of credits](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/). This applies for admissions starting from winter semester 2022/23.

You can see that your degree programme has been closed in u:space at Studies > Study overview.

The [resumption of studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/) is possible within the corresponding period, provided you fulfil all requirements for the degree programme. By resuming your studies you are transferred to the current curriculum. Therefore, it may be possible that not all examinations you have already completed are recognised.

reranked.text: will lose your admission to the program if you do not achieve the minimum number of credits by the deadline. - Do I also have to complete the minimum number of credits when I resume my studies? Students of all bachelor's and diploma programmes who begin or [resume](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/) their studies in the winter semester of 2022/2023 or later must obtain the minimum number of credits. The minimum number of credits must be obtained once within four semesters. If you interrupt your studies (again) during these four semesters, the ECTS credits and semesters are counted after you resume your studies. However, once the minimum number of credits has been obtained, it does not have to be obtained again for this degree programme. For example: You started your studies in the winter semester of 2022/23, studied for 2 semesters and obtained 12 ECTS credits. You interrupt your studies in the following semester. You resume your studies in the summer semester of 2024. You still have 2 semesters to obtain the missing 4 ECTS credits - i.e. until one month after the end of the fourth semester at the latest. If you interrupt your studies again after that, you do not have to prove again that you have obtained the minimum number of credits in this study programme again. selected.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
selected.text:
You can end your studies in the following ways:

- successfully completing your degree programme and graduating
- closing one or multiple degree programme(s) (formerly called "exmatriculation")

Closing degree programmes is possible all year round. This is how you proceed:

1. Deregister from all courses with continous assessment and examinations. This is possible via u:space during the deregistration period. Afterwards, please contact the lecturers directly. Closing your studies does not qualify as an important reason for the cancellation of an examination (info only available in German).
2. In case, return books to the University Library or institute libraries. You can only withdraw (= close all degree programmes at the University of Vienna) if you have not borrowed any books.
3. If you close a bachelor-/diploma or master programme, please message us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/category/stuz) and specify which degree programme you want to close. If you want to close a doctoral/PhD programme, please contact [doktorat.zulassung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0bwncuuwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).
4. After a few days, we will inform you about the processing of your application. Check the changed status of your degree programme in u:space at Studies > Study overview. You can download your confirmation of withdrawal at Personal matters > My documents.

selected.title: Closing a degree programme/Withdrawing from the University of Vienna
selected.text:
The admission to the degree programme expires and your degree programme is closed if:

- You have successfully completed your degree programme. An admission for the final administrative steps carried out by the StudiesServicecenter (SSC) is not required. The admission ends with the date of the last examination.
- You do not pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee. You can also not pay on purpose and thereby discontinue your studies and withdraw from the university.
- If you have paid at another Austrian university/college of teacher education ("Pädagogische Hochschule") and do not re-enroll your studies at the University of Vienna.
- If you do not achieve the [minimum number of credits](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/). This applies for admissions starting from winter semester 2022/23.

You can see that your degree programme has been closed in u:space at Studies > Study overview.

The [resumption of studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/) is possible within the corresponding period, provided you fulfil all requirements for the degree programme. By resuming your studies you are transferred to the current curriculum. Therefore, it may be possible that not all examinations you have already completed are recognised.

  If you want to take further exams or receive examination results, wait until all examination results have been entered by the [StudiesServiceCenter](https://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) of your degree programme and appear in u:space. After a degree programme has been closed, it is neither possible to take exams nor to enter these examination results.
- When can I close my degree proramme(s)?

  You can close your degree programme(s) year-round via our [Servicdesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/category/stuz).
